input.content:
journey:
  name: semantic-verification
  jobs: [J1, J4]
  description: Event-triggered LLM reasoning for semantic contradiction detection

  actors:
    - name: surrealdb_event
      type: system
    - name: observer_route
      type: system
    - name: verification_pipeline
      type: system
    - name: llm_reasoning
      type: agent
      model: haiku-class
    - name: graph
      type: datastore
    - name: user
      type: human

  steps:
    - id: state_transition
      actor: surrealdb_event
      action: Fires ASYNC EVENT on entity state change
      produces: [entity_id, entity_table, entity_body]
      emotion: null

    - id: dispatch
      actor: observer_route
      action: Validates table allowlist + UUID, loads entity from DB
      consumes: [entity_id, entity_table, entity_body]
      produces: [entity_record]
      error: Entity not found -> 404, skip

    - id: gather_signals
      actor: verification_pipeline
      action: Load related decisions, constraints, commits, external signals
      consumes: [entity_record]
      produces: [related_decisions, constraints, external_signals, deterministic_verdict]
      error: External signals unavailable -> proceed with graph-only context

    - id: llm_reason
      actor: llm_reasoning
      action: Evaluate semantic contradiction with structured output
      consumes: [entity_record, related_decisions, constraints, external_signals, deterministic_verdict]
      produces: [llm_verdict, reasoning_text, confidence, evidence_refs]
      decisions:
        - condition: deterministic_verdict == match AND high_confidence
          action: Skip LLM call, use deterministic result
        - condition: llm_confidence < threshold
          action: Downgrade to inconclusive
      error: LLM failure -> fall back to deterministic verdict

    - id: create_observation
      actor: verification_pipeline
      action: Persist observation with verdict, link via observes edge
      consumes: [llm_verdict, reasoning_text, confidence, evidence_refs, entity_record]
      produces: [observation_id]

    - id: surface
      actor: user
      action: Reads observation in feed, chat context, or agent prompt
      consumes: [observation_id]
      emotion: confidence

  shared_artifacts:
    - name: entity_record
      source: dispatch
      consumers: [gather_signals, llm_reason, create_observation]
    - name: related_decisions
      source: gather_signals
      consumers: [llm_reason]
    - name: deterministic_verdict
      source: gather_signals
      consumers: [llm_reason]
    - name: llm_verdict
      source: llm_reason
      consumers: [create_observation, surface]

journey:
  name: pattern-synthesis
  jobs: [J3]
  description: Graph-scan LLM synthesis of cross-signal patterns

  steps:
    - id: trigger_scan
      actor: trigger
      action: Manual or scheduled POST /api/observe/scan/:workspaceId

    - id: collect_anomalies
      actor: graph_scan
      action: Run deterministic queries for contradictions, stale blockers, drift
      produces: [anomalies]
      error: No anomalies -> skip LLM, return empty

    - id: synthesize_patterns
      actor: llm_reasoning
      action: Correlate anomalies into named patterns with evidence
      consumes: [anomalies]
      produces: [patterns]
      constraints:
        - min_signals_per_pattern: 2
        - pattern_vocabulary: [bottleneck_decision, cascade_block, priority_drift, stale_cluster, contradiction_cluster]
      error: LLM failure -> return anomalies without synthesis

    - id: create_synthesis_observations
      actor: graph_scan
      action: Create observation per pattern, link to all contributing entities
      consumes: [patterns]
      produces: [synthesis_observation_ids]
      dedup: Check existing open synthesis observations before creating

    - id: surface
      actor: user
      action: Read synthesis observations in feed
      consumes: [synthesis_observation_ids]
      emotion: insight

input.content:
Feature: Semantic Verification via LLM Reasoning
  As the Brain knowledge graph system
  I want the observer to use LLM reasoning for semantic contradiction detection
  So that contradictions between decisions and implementation are caught beyond string matching

  Background:
    Given a workspace with an active observer agent
    And a Haiku-class LLM model configured for observer reasoning

  # --- J1: Semantic Contradiction Detection ---

  Scenario: LLM detects semantic contradiction between decision and completed task
    Given a confirmed decision "minimize external dependencies"
    And a task "add Redis, Kafka, and RabbitMQ integrations" linked to the same project
    When the task status transitions to "completed"
    And the observer verification pipeline runs
    Then the LLM reasoning step receives the decision text and task details
    And the LLM identifies a semantic contradiction
    And an observation is created with severity "conflict"
    And the observation text explains the contradiction in natural language
    And the observation links to both the decision and the task via observes edges

  Scenario: LLM confirms no contradiction for semantically aligned entities
    Given a confirmed decision "use TypeScript for all backend services"
    And a task "implement auth middleware in TypeScript" linked to the same project
    When the task status transitions to "completed"
    And the observer verification pipeline runs
    Then the LLM reasoning step finds no semantic contradiction
    And an observation is created with severity "info" and verified true
    And the observation text confirms alignment

  Scenario: Deterministic match skips LLM call for cost optimization
    Given a task with a linked commit
    And the commit has passing CI status from GitHub
    And the deterministic verification returns "match" with high confidence
    When the observer verification pipeline runs
    Then the LLM reasoning step is skipped
    And an observation is created using the deterministic verdict
    And no LLM API call is made

  Scenario: LLM call failure falls back to deterministic verdict
    Given a task completion event triggers the observer
    And the LLM service is unavailable
    When the observer verification pipeline runs
    Then the pipeline falls back to the deterministic verdict
    And an observation is created with the deterministic result
    And the observation source indicates "deterministic_fallback"

  Scenario: Low-confidence LLM verdict downgrades to inconclusive
    Given a task completion event with ambiguous relationship to decisions
    When the LLM reasoning step produces a verdict with confidence below threshold
    Then the observation is created with verdict "inconclusive"
    And the severity is "info" rather than "conflict"

  # --- J3: Cross-Signal Pattern Synthesis ---

  Scenario: LLM synthesizes bottleneck pattern from multiple blocked tasks
    Given a workspace with 3 tasks blocked on the same unresolved decision
    When a graph scan runs on the workspace
    Then the LLM synthesis step identifies a "bottleneck_decision" pattern
    And a synthesis observation is created with observation_type "pattern"
    And the observation links to the decision and all 3 blocked tasks
    And the observation text names the bottleneck and suggests resolution

  Scenario: LLM synthesizes cascade block pattern
    Given task A is blocked by task B
    And task B is blocked by task C
    And task C has been blocked for 20 days
    When a graph scan runs
    Then the LLM identifies a "cascade_block" pattern
    And the synthesis observation explains the chain and impact

  Scenario: Synthesis requires minimum 2 contributing signals
    Given a workspace with only 1 stale blocked task
    When a graph scan runs
    Then no synthesis pattern is created for that single anomaly
    And the individual anomaly is still reported as a standard observation

  Scenario: Synthesis deduplicates against existing open patterns
    Given an existing open observation of type "pattern" for "bottleneck_decision" on decision X
    And the same 3 tasks are still blocked on decision X
    When a graph scan runs
    Then no duplicate synthesis observation is created

  Scenario: Graph scan with no anomalies skips LLM entirely
    Given a healthy workspace with no contradictions, stale blockers, or drift
    When a graph scan runs
    Then no LLM synthesis call is made
    And the scan returns an empty result

  # --- J2: Reasoning-Quality Peer Review ---

  Scenario: LLM evaluates reasoning quality of peer observation
    Given agent "pm_agent" created an observation claiming "task X is at risk"
    And the observation cites 2 evidence references in the graph
    When the observer peer review triggers
    Then the LLM evaluates whether the claim follows from the evidence
    And the observer creates a review observation linked to the original

  # --- J4: Contextual Natural Language (implicit in all scenarios) ---
  # Note: All LLM-produced observations inherently satisfy J4 by generating
  # contextual, actionable text rather than template strings.

    And the observer verification pipeline runs
    Then the LLM reasoning step finds no semantic contradiction
    And an observation is created with severity "info" and verified true
    And the observation text confirms alignment

  Scenario: Deterministic match skips LLM call for cost optimization
    Given a task with a linked commit
    And the commit has passing CI status from GitHub
    And the deterministic verification returns "match" with high confidence
    When the observer verification pipeline runs
    Then the LLM reasoning step is skipped
    And an observation is created using the deterministic verdict
    And no LLM API call is made

  Scenario: LLM call failure falls back to deterministic verdict
    Given a task completion event triggers the observer
    And the LLM service is unavailable
    When the observer verification pipeline runs
    Then the pipeline falls back to the deterministic verdict
    And an observation is created with the deterministic result
    And the observation source indicates "deterministic_fallback"

  Scenario: Low-confidence LLM verdict downgrades to inconclusive
    Given a task completion event with ambiguous relationship to decisions
    When the LLM reasoning step produces a verdict with confidence below threshold
    Then the observation is created with verdict "inconclusive"
    And the severity is "info" rather than "conflict"

  # --- J3: Cross-Signal Pattern Synthesis ---

  Scenario: LLM synthesizes bottleneck pattern from multiple blocked tasks
    Given a workspace with 3 tasks blocked on the same unresolved decision
    When a graph scan runs on the workspace
    Then the LLM synthesis step identifies a "bottleneck_decision" pattern
    And a synthesis observation is created with observation_type "pattern"
    And the observation links to the decision and all 3 blocked tasks
    And the observation text names the bottleneck and suggests resolution

  Scenario: LLM synthesizes cascade block pattern
    Given task A is blocked by task B
    And task B is blocked by task C
    And task C has been blocked for 20 days
    When a graph scan runs
    Then the LLM identifies a "cascade_block" pattern
    And the synthesis observation explains the chain and impact

  Scenario: Synthesis requires minimum 2 contributing signals
    Given a workspace with only 1 stale blocked task
    When a graph scan runs
    Then no synthesis pattern is created for that single anomaly
    And the individual anomaly is still reported as a standard observation

  Scenario: Synthesis deduplicates against existing open patterns
    Given an existing open observation of type "pattern" for "bottleneck_decision" on decision X
    And the same 3 tasks are still blocked on decision X
    When a graph scan runs
    Then no duplicate synthesis observation is created

  Scenario: Graph scan with no anomalies skips LLM entirely
    Given a healthy workspace with no contradictions, stale blockers, or drift
    When a graph scan runs
    Then no LLM synthesis call is made
    And the scan returns an empty result

  # --- J2: Reasoning-Quality Peer Review ---

  Scenario: LLM evaluates reasoning quality of peer observation
    Given agent "pm_agent" created an observation claiming "task X is at risk"
    And the observation cites 2 evidence references in the graph
    When the observer peer review triggers
    Then the LLM evaluates whether the claim follows from the evidence
    And the observer creates a review observation linked to the original

  # --- J4: Contextual Natural Language (implicit in all scenarios) ---
  # Note: All LLM-produced observations inherently satisfy J4 by generating
  # contextual, actionable text rather than template strings.

input.content:
# Shared Artifacts Registry: Observer LLM Reasoning

## Artifact Definitions

| Artifact | Type | Source | Consumers | Lifecycle |
|----------|------|--------|-----------|-----------|
| `${entity_record}` | DB record | observer-route (DB lookup) | verification-pipeline, llm-reasoning | Per-event |
| `${related_decisions}` | Decision[] | verification-pipeline (graph query) | llm-reasoning | Per-event |
| `${constraints}` | Constraint[] | verification-pipeline (graph query) | llm-reasoning | Per-event |
| `${external_signals}` | Signal[] | external-signals (GitHub API) | verification-pipeline, llm-reasoning | Per-event, may be empty |
| `${deterministic_verdict}` | Verdict | verification-pipeline | llm-reasoning (context), observation (fallback) | Per-event |
| `${llm_verdict}` | StructuredVerdict | llm-reasoning | observation creation | Per-event, nullable on LLM failure |
| `${anomalies}` | Anomaly[] | graph-scan (deterministic queries) | llm-synthesis | Per-scan |
| `${patterns}` | Pattern[] | llm-synthesis | observation creation | Per-scan, may be empty |
| `${observation_id}` | RecordId | DB create | feed, chat context, agent prompts | Persistent |

## Structured Output Schemas

### LLM Verification Verdict

```typescript
type LlmVerdict = {
  verdict: "match" | "mismatch" | "inconclusive";
  confidence: number;        // 0.0 - 1.0
  reasoning: string;         // Natural language explanation (J4)
  evidence_refs: string[];   // Entity IDs referenced in reasoning
  contradiction?: {
    claim: string;           // What the entity claims
    reality: string;         // What the evidence shows
  };
};
```

### LLM Synthesis Pattern

```typescript
type SynthesisPattern = {
  pattern_name: "bottleneck_decision" | "cascade_block" | "priority_drift" | "stale_cluster" | "contradiction_cluster";
  description: string;         // Natural language synthesis
  contributing_entities: string[]; // Entity IDs (minimum 2)
  severity: "warning" | "conflict";
  suggested_action: string;    // Recommended resolution
};
```

## Data Flow Diagram

```
EVENT (state transition)
  |
  v
observer-route
  |-- validate table + UUID
  |-- load ${entity_record}
  |
  v
verification-pipeline
  |-- load ${related_decisions}, ${constraints}
  |-- gather ${external_signals}
  |-- compute ${deterministic_verdict}
  |
  |-- [if deterministic high-confidence match] --> skip LLM
  |
  v
llm-reasoning
  |-- input: entity + decisions + constraints + signals + deterministic verdict
  |-- output: ${llm_verdict} (structured)
  |-- [on failure] --> use ${deterministic_verdict}
  |
  v
create observation
  |-- verdict text from ${llm_verdict}.reasoning OR deterministic template
  |-- severity from verdict
  |-- link via observes edge
  |
  v
${observation_id} --> feed / chat / agent context


SCAN (manual or scheduled)
  |
  v
graph-scan (deterministic)
  |-- contradictions, stale blockers, drift
  |-- output: ${anomalies}
  |
  |-- [if empty] --> return, skip LLM
  |
  v
llm-synthesis
  |-- input: ${anomalies} + workspace context
  |-- output: ${patterns} (min 2 signals each)
  |-- [on failure] --> return ${anomalies} without synthesis
  |
  v
create synthesis observations
  |-- one per pattern
  |-- link to all contributing entities
  |-- dedup against existing open patterns
```

input.content:
# Requirements: Observer LLM Reasoning

## R1 — LLM Verification Pipeline Integration

The observer verification pipeline must support an optional LLM reasoning step that evaluates semantic contradictions between entities and their related decisions/constraints.

**Traces to:** J1 (Semantic Contradiction Detection)

**Constraints:**
- LLM call is optional — deterministic pipeline remains the fallback
- LLM receives structured context (entity + decisions + constraints + signals), never raw graph queries
- LLM produces structured output (verdict, confidence, reasoning, evidence refs)
- Confidence threshold determines observation severity (below threshold = inconclusive)

## R2 — Cost-Optimized LLM Invocation

The pipeline must skip the LLM call when the deterministic verification already produces a high-confidence result, to control per-event cost.

**Traces to:** J1 (Four Forces — Anxiety: cost at scale)

**Constraints:**
- Define skip criteria: deterministic verdict == "match" AND external signals confirm
- Track LLM call count and skip rate for observability
- Use Haiku-class model (cheapest capable model)

## R3 — LLM Fallback on Failure

When the LLM call fails (timeout, rate limit, service unavailable), the pipeline must fall back to the deterministic verdict without blocking or erroring.

**Traces to:** J1 (Four Forces — Anxiety: LLM reliability)

**Constraints:**
- Timeout per LLM call (configurable, default 10s)
- Fallback observation includes source "deterministic_fallback" for tracking
- No retry within the same event (SurrealDB EVENT RETRY handles full-pipeline retry)

## R4 — Cross-Signal Pattern Synthesis

The graph scan must support an LLM synthesis step that correlates individual anomalies into named patterns with minimum evidence thresholds.

**Traces to:** J3 (Cross-Signal Pattern Synthesis)

**Constraints:**
- Minimum 2 contributing signals per pattern
- Pattern names from controlled vocabulary (extensible enum)
- Synthesis observations link to all contributing entities via observes edges
- Deduplication against existing open pattern observations

## R5 — Structured LLM Output Schema

All LLM interactions must use structured output (JSON schema) to ensure parseable, validatable results and prevent hallucinated entity references.

**Traces to:** J1, J3 (Four Forces — Anxiety: hallucination)

**Constraints:**
- Verification verdict schema: verdict, confidence, reasoning, evidence_refs, optional contradiction
- Synthesis pattern schema: pattern_name (enum), description, contributing_entities, severity, suggested_action
- Post-validation: strip evidence_refs that don't resolve to real entity IDs

## R6 — LLM Reasoning Peer Review

The observer's peer review path (triggered by observation_peer_review EVENT) must use LLM reasoning to evaluate whether a non-observer observation's claim follows from its cited evidence.

**Traces to:** J2 (Reasoning-Quality Peer Review)

**Constraints:**
- Only reviews observations that cite evidence (have observes edges)
- Creates a review observation linked to the reviewed observation
- Does not modify or delete the reviewed observation
- Cascade prevention unchanged (source_agent != "observer_agent" guard)

## R7 — Model Configuration

The observer LLM model must be configurable via environment variable, consistent with the existing model configuration pattern.

**Traces to:** All jobs (infrastructure)

**Constraints:**
- Environment variable: `OBSERVER_MODEL` (follows `CHAT_AGENT_MODEL`, `PM_AGENT_MODEL` pattern)
- Default: Haiku-class model
- Model client reuses existing inference provider abstraction (OpenRouter/Ollama)

input.content:
# User Stories: Observer LLM Reasoning

All stories trace to JTBD job stories in `docs/ux/observer-llm-reasoning/jtbd-job-stories.md`.

---

## US-1: Semantic Contradiction Detection on Task Completion

**As** a workspace owner,
**I want** the observer to use LLM reasoning to detect semantic contradictions when a task is completed,
**so that** I catch misalignment between decisions and implementation that string matching would miss.

**Job trace:** J1
**Requirements:** R1, R2, R3, R5, R7
**Size:** M

### Acceptance Criteria
- AC-1.1: When a task completion triggers the observer, and related decisions exist, the LLM evaluates semantic alignment
- AC-1.2: When a semantic contradiction is found, the observation has severity "conflict" and links to both decision and task
- AC-1.3: When the deterministic check returns high-confidence "match", the LLM call is skipped
- AC-1.4: When the LLM call fails, the pipeline falls back to the deterministic verdict
- AC-1.5: When the LLM confidence is below threshold, the verdict is "inconclusive" with severity "info"

---

## US-2: Semantic Contradiction Detection on Decision Confirmation

**As** a workspace owner,
**I want** the observer to evaluate newly confirmed decisions against existing completed tasks,
**so that** confirming a new decision surfaces contradictions with already-shipped work.

**Job trace:** J1
**Requirements:** R1, R5, R7
**Size:** S

### Acceptance Criteria
- AC-2.1: When a decision is confirmed, the observer loads completed tasks in the same project
- AC-2.2: The LLM evaluates whether any completed task contradicts the newly confirmed decision
- AC-2.3: Contradictions create observations linking the decision and the contradicting task(s)

---

## US-3: Cross-Signal Pattern Synthesis in Graph Scan

**As** a workspace owner,
**I want** the graph scan to synthesize individual anomalies into named patterns,
**so that** I see systemic issues (bottlenecks, cascade blocks) instead of isolated findings.

**Job trace:** J3
**Requirements:** R4, R5, R7
**Size:** L

### Acceptance Criteria
- AC-3.1: After deterministic scan, anomalies are passed to LLM for pattern synthesis
- AC-3.2: Each pattern has a name from the controlled vocabulary and links to 2+ contributing entities
- AC-3.3: Synthesis observations have observation_type "pattern"
- AC-3.4: Existing open pattern observations for the same entities are not duplicated
- AC-3.5: When no anomalies are found, the LLM is not called
- AC-3.6: When the LLM fails, deterministic anomalies are returned without synthesis

---

## US-4: LLM-Enhanced Peer Review

**As** a workspace owner,
**I want** the observer to evaluate the reasoning quality of observations created by other agents,
**so that** I can distinguish well-grounded signals from noise.

**Job trace:** J2
**Requirements:** R6, R5, R7
**Size:** M

### Acceptance Criteria
- AC-4.1: When a non-observer observation with evidence links is created, the LLM evaluates the reasoning
- AC-4.2: The review observation links to the reviewed observation via observes edge
- AC-4.3: The review does not modify or delete the original observation
- AC-4.4: Observer's own observations do not trigger peer review (cascade prevention)

---

## US-5: Observer Model Configuration

**As** a platform operator,
**I want** to configure which LLM model the observer uses via environment variable,
**so that** I can control cost and performance independently of other agents.

**Job trace:** All (infrastructure)
**Requirements:** R7
**Size:** S

### Acceptance Criteria
- AC-5.1: `OBSERVER_MODEL` env var configures the model used for observer reasoning
- AC-5.2: When `OBSERVER_MODEL` is not set, LLM reasoning is disabled and the pipeline uses deterministic-only mode
- AC-5.3: The model client uses the existing inference provider abstraction (OpenRouter/Ollama)

input.content:
# Acceptance Criteria: Observer LLM Reasoning

## AC-1: Semantic Verification Pipeline (US-1, US-2)

### AC-1.1: LLM reasoning invoked for task completion with related decisions
```gherkin
Given a workspace with a confirmed decision "minimize external dependencies"
And a task "add Redis and Kafka" linked to the same project
When the task status transitions to "completed"
Then the observer loads related decisions for the task's project
And invokes the LLM with entity context and decision text
And the LLM returns a structured verdict
```

### AC-1.2: Contradiction creates conflict observation with entity links
```gherkin
Given the LLM returns verdict "mismatch" with confidence >= threshold
Then an observation is created with:
  | field             | value                    |
  | severity          | conflict                 |
  | observation_type  | contradiction            |
  | source_agent      | observer_agent           |
  | verified          | false                    |
  | source            | llm                      |
And an observes edge links the observation to the triggering task
And an observes edge links the observation to the contradicted decision
And the observation text contains the LLM's natural language reasoning
```

### AC-1.3: High-confidence deterministic match skips LLM
```gherkin
Given a task with a linked commit
And the commit has passing CI from GitHub
When the deterministic verification returns "match"
Then the LLM reasoning step is not invoked
And the observation is created with source "github" (not "llm")
```

### AC-1.4: LLM failure falls back to deterministic verdict
```gherkin
Given the LLM service returns an error (timeout, 500, rate limit)
Then the pipeline creates an observation using the deterministic verdict
And the observation source is "deterministic_fallback"
And no error is propagated to the caller
```

### AC-1.5: Low confidence downgrades to inconclusive
```gherkin
Given the LLM returns a verdict with confidence < 0.5
Then the observation verdict is "inconclusive"
And the severity is "info"
And the observation is still created (not discarded)
```

### AC-1.6: Decision confirmation checks against completed tasks
```gherkin
Given 2 completed tasks in project P
When decision D is confirmed in project P
Then the observer loads both completed tasks
And the LLM evaluates each for contradiction against decision D
And any contradictions create linked observations
```

## AC-2: Pattern Synthesis (US-3)

### AC-2.1: Anomalies passed to LLM after deterministic scan
```gherkin
Given a workspace scan finds 5 anomalies (2 contradictions, 2 stale blockers, 1 drift)
Then all 5 anomalies are passed to the LLM synthesis step
And the LLM returns 0 or more named patterns
```

### AC-2.2: Pattern requires minimum 2 contributing entities
```gherkin
Given the LLM proposes a pattern with only 1 contributing entity
Then that pattern is discarded
And no synthesis observation is created for it
```

### AC-2.3: Pattern observation links to all contributing entities
```gherkin
Given the LLM identifies a "bottleneck_decision" pattern
With contributing entities [decision:D1, task:T1, task:T2, task:T3]
Then an observation is created with observation_type "pattern"
And 4 observes edges link the observation to D1, T1, T2, T3
```

### AC-2.4: Deduplication prevents repeated pattern observations
```gherkin
Given an open observation of type "pattern" exists
And it links to the same set of contributing entities
When a new scan detects the same pattern
Then no duplicate observation is created
```

### AC-2.5: Empty anomaly list skips LLM
```gherkin
Given a workspace scan finds 0 anomalies
Then the LLM synthesis step is not invoked
And the scan returns an empty result
```

### AC-2.6: LLM synthesis failure returns anomalies without patterns
```gherkin
Given a workspace scan finds 3 anomalies
And the LLM synthesis call fails
Then the 3 anomalies are returned as individual observations (current behavior)
And no synthesis pattern observations are created
```

## AC-3: Peer Review (US-4)

### AC-3.1: LLM evaluates observation reasoning
```gherkin
Given pm_agent creates an observation "task X is at risk"
And the observation has observes edges to 2 entities
When the observation_peer_review EVENT fires
Then the observer loads the observation and its linked entities
And the LLM evaluates whether the claim follows from the evidence
```

### AC-3.2: Review creates linked observation
```gherkin
Given the LLM peer review is complete
Then a new observation is created by observer_agent
And it links to the reviewed observation via an observes edge
And the text describes the review finding
```

### AC-3.3: Original observation is not modified
```gherkin
Given a peer review completes
Then the original observation's text, severity, status, and links are unchanged
```

## AC-4: Configuration (US-5)

### AC-4.1: OBSERVER_MODEL configures LLM
```gherkin
Given OBSERVER_MODEL is set to "anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"
When the observer invokes LLM reasoning
Then the configured model is used for the API call
```

### AC-4.2: Missing OBSERVER_MODEL disables LLM reasoning
```gherkin
Given OBSERVER_MODEL is not set in the environment
When the observer verification pipeline runs
Then only deterministic verification is performed
And no LLM API calls are made
```

### AC-4.3: Model uses existing inference provider
```gherkin
Given INFERENCE_PROVIDER is "openrouter"
And OBSERVER_MODEL is set
Then the observer uses the OpenRouter client for LLM calls
```

toolUseResult.content:
# Acceptance Criteria: Observer LLM Reasoning

## AC-1: Semantic Verification Pipeline (US-1, US-2)

### AC-1.1: LLM reasoning invoked for task completion with related decisions
```gherkin
Given a workspace with a confirmed decision "minimize external dependencies"
And a task "add Redis and Kafka" linked to the same project
When the task status transitions to "completed"
Then the observer loads related decisions for the task's project
And invokes the LLM with entity context and decision text
And the LLM returns a structured verdict
```

### AC-1.2: Contradiction creates conflict observation with entity links
```gherkin
Given the LLM returns verdict "mismatch" with confidence >= threshold
Then an observation is created with:
  | field             | value                    |
  | severity          | conflict                 |
  | observation_type  | contradiction            |
  | source_agent      | observer_agent           |
  | verified          | false                    |
  | source            | llm                      |
And an observes edge links the observation to the triggering task
And an observes edge links the observation to the contradicted decision
And the observation text contains the LLM's natural language reasoning
```

### AC-1.3: High-confidence deterministic match skips LLM
```gherkin
Given a task with a linked commit
And the commit has passing CI from GitHub
When the deterministic verification returns "match"
Then the LLM reasoning step is not invoked
And the observation is created with source "github" (not "llm")
```

### AC-1.4: LLM failure falls back to deterministic verdict
```gherkin
Given the LLM service returns an error (timeout, 500, rate limit)
Then the pipeline creates an observation using the deterministic verdict
And the observation source is "deterministic_fallback"
And no error is propagated to the caller
```

### AC-1.5: Low confidence downgrades to inconclusive
```gherkin
Given the LLM returns a verdict with confidence < 0.5
Then the observation verdict is "inconclusive"
And the severity is "info"
And the observation is still created (not discarded)
```

### AC-1.6: Decision confirmation checks against completed tasks
```gherkin
Given 2 completed tasks in project P
When decision D is confirmed in project P
Then the observer loads both completed tasks
And the LLM evaluates each for contradiction against decision D
And any contradictions create linked observations
```

## AC-2: Pattern Synthesis (US-3)

### AC-2.1: Anomalies passed to LLM after deterministic scan
```gherkin
Given a workspace scan finds 5 anomalies (2 contradictions, 2 stale blockers, 1 drift)
Then all 5 anomalies are passed to the LLM synthesis step
And the LLM returns 0 or more named patterns
```

### AC-2.2: Pattern requires minimum 2 contributing entities
```gherkin
Given the LLM proposes a pattern with only 1 contributing entity
Then that pattern is discarded
And no synthesis observation is created for it
```

### AC-2.3: Pattern observation links to all contributing entities
```gherkin
Given the LLM identifies a "bottleneck_decision" pattern
With contributing entities [decision:D1, task:T1, task:T2, task:T3]
Then an observation is created with observation_type "pattern"
And 4 observes edges link the observation to D1, T1, T2, T3
```

### AC-2.4: Deduplication prevents repeated pattern observations
```gherkin
Given an open observation of type "pattern" exists
And it links to the same set of contributing entities
When a new scan detects the same pattern
Then no duplicate observation is created
```

### AC-2.5: Empty anomaly list skips LLM
```gherkin
Given a workspace scan finds 0 anomalies
Then the LLM synthesis step is not invoked
And the scan returns an empty result
```

### AC-2.6: LLM synthesis failure returns anomalies without patterns
```gherkin
Given a workspace scan finds 3 anomalies
And the LLM synthesis call fails
Then the 3 anomalies are returned as individual observations (current behavior)
And no synthesis pattern observations are created
```

## AC-3: Peer Review (US-4)

### AC-3.1: LLM evaluates observation reasoning
```gherkin
Given pm_agent creates an observation "task X is at risk"
And the observation has observes edges to 2 entities
When the observation_peer_review EVENT fires
Then the observer loads the observation and its linked entities
And the LLM evaluates whether the claim follows from the evidence
```

### AC-3.2: Review creates linked observation
```gherkin
Given the LLM peer review is complete
Then a new observation is created by observer_agent
And it links to the reviewed observation via an observes edge
And the text describes the review finding
```

### AC-3.3: Original observation is not modified
```gherkin
Given a peer review completes
Then the original observation's text, severity, status, and links are unchanged
```

## AC-4: Configuration (US-5)

### AC-4.1: OBSERVER_MODEL configures LLM
```gherkin
Given OBSERVER_MODEL is set to "anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"
When the observer invokes LLM reasoning
Then the configured model is used for the API call
```

### AC-4.2: Missing OBSERVER_MODEL disables LLM reasoning
```gherkin
Given OBSERVER_MODEL is not set in the environment
When the observer verification pipeline runs
Then only deterministic verification is performed
And no LLM API calls are made
```

### AC-4.3: Model uses existing inference provider
```gherkin
Given INFERENCE_PROVIDER is "openrouter"
And OBSERVER_MODEL is set
Then the observer uses the OpenRouter client for LLM calls
```

# Definition of Ready Checklist: Observer LLM Reasoning

## DoR Items

### 1. User stories clearly defined
- [x] 5 user stories with role/want/so-that format
- [x] Each story sized (S/M/L)
- [x] Stories are independent and can be delivered incrementally
- **Evidence:** `docs/requirements/observer-llm-reasoning/user-stories.md`

### 2. Acceptance criteria testable
- [x] All AC in Given-When-Then format
- [x] Each AC maps to a specific assertion (no subjective criteria)
- [x] AC covers happy path, error paths, and edge cases
- **Evidence:** `docs/requirements/observer-llm-reasoning/acceptance-criteria.md`

### 3. JTBD analysis complete
- [x] 4 job stories in job story format
- [x] Job dimensions (functional/emotional/social) for each
- [x] Four forces analysis for each job
- [x] Opportunity scoring with ranked priorities
- **Evidence:** `docs/ux/observer-llm-reasoning/jtbd-*.md`

### 4. UX journey mapped
- [x] 2 journeys (semantic verification, pattern synthesis)
- [x] Visual journey maps with emotional arc
- [x] YAML schema with shared artifacts
- [x] Gherkin scenarios (12 scenarios)
- **Evidence:** `docs/ux/observer-llm-reasoning/journey-*.md`, `.yaml`, `.feature`

### 5. Dependencies identified
- [x] Existing observer pipeline (app/src/server/observer/) — in place
- [x] ToolLoopAgent scaffolding (app/src/server/agents/observer/) — in place
- [x] Model client abstraction — in place (used by chat agent, PM agent)
- [x] SurrealDB EVENTs — in place (5 events, all tested)
- [x] No external dependencies required
- **Evidence:** Codebase exploration confirmed all infrastructure exists

### 6. Shared artifacts tracked
- [x] Artifact registry with source/consumer mapping
- [x] Structured output schemas defined (LlmVerdict, SynthesisPattern)
- [x] Data flow diagram
- **Evidence:** `docs/ux/observer-llm-reasoning/shared-artifacts-registry.md`

### 7. Risks and mitigations documented
- [x] LLM hallucination → structured output + post-validation
- [x] Cost at scale → skip criteria + Haiku model
- [x] Latency → already async (ASYNC events)
- [x] Alert fatigue → minimum signal thresholds + dedup
- [x] LLM failure → deterministic fallback
- **Evidence:** `docs/ux/observer-llm-reasoning/jtbd-opportunity-scores.md` § Risk Mitigation

### 8. Stories traceable to jobs
- [x] Every user story traces to at least one job
- [x] US-1 → J1, US-2 → J1, US-3 → J3, US-4 → J2, US-5 → All
- [x] J4 satisfied implicitly via LLM-generated text in US-1/2/3/4
- **Evidence:** Job trace annotations in `user-stories.md`

## Verdict: READY

All 8 DoR items pass. Feature is ready for DESIGN wave handoff.

input.prompt:
Review the DISCUSS wave artifacts for the "Observer LLM Reasoning" feature. Validate:

1. Journey coherence and emotional arc quality

3. Definition of Ready checklist compliance
4. LeanUX antipatterns

- docs/ux/observer-llm-reasoning/ (all files)
- docs/requirements/observer-llm-reasoning/ (all files)

This is a cross-cutting backend/agent feature extending an existing observer agent with LLM-based reasoning for semantic contradiction detection and pattern synthesis. No UI changes.

Check for:
- Any DoR item that should fail

Now I have all the artifacts. Let me conduct the comprehensive review.

```yaml

  artifact_reviewed: "docs/ux/observer-llm-reasoning, docs/requirements/observer-llm-reasoning"
  review_date: "2026-03-11T00:00:00Z"
  reviewer: "nw-product-owner-reviewer (Eclipse)"

  journey_review:
    journey_coherence:
      - issue: "Pattern synthesis journey missing explicit dedup logic step"
        severity: "medium"
        location: "journey-pattern-synthesis-visual.md, Step 4"

        severity: "low"

Both are valid but should be explicitly mapped to emotional state progression (frustration→relief→confidence or uncertainty→clarity→insight). Add step-by-step emotion annotations for all 6/5 steps respectively."

        severity: "low"
        location: "journey-semantic-verification-visual.md"
        recommendation: "Add emotion label for gather signals step (e.g., 'Anticipation — building context')."
    
    shared_artifacts:

Current doc says 'verification-pipeline (graph query)' but doesn't specify scope (task-scoped vs project-scoped vs workspace-scoped). Recommend: 'Loaded per event for task's project via graph query SELECT FROM constraint WHERE project = $task.project.'"
      
      - issue: "Deduplication logic for pattern synthesis not formalized as shared artifact"
        severity: "medium"

        recommendation: "Add artifact ${existing_open_patterns}: Type=Observation[], Source=graph_scan (query for open observations with type='pattern'), Consumer=create_synthesis_observations (dedup check). Formalize query: 'SELECT FROM observation WHERE type='pattern' AND status='open' AND observes->{set of pattern.contributing_entities}'"
    
    example_data:

        recommendation: "Provide example: 'Decision text: \"Architectural decision: Minimize external service dependencies to reduce operational complexity and vendor lock-in. Prefer in-process alternatives (Redis, SQLite) over cloud services (Datadog, Slack API) except where unavoidable. Target: <5 external service calls per user session.\"' Show both the constraint text and the decision body."
      
      - issue: "Task completion example missing implementation context (what was actually built)"
        severity: "medium"
        location: "journey-semantic-verification.feature, line 14"
        integration_risk: "Without commit details or task description showing what was actually added, LLM can't reason about semantic alignment. Risk: hallucinated contradictions based on vague task names."
        recommendation: "Expand to: 'Task: \"Add Redis, Kafka, RabbitMQ integrations\" with linked commits abc1234 and def5678. Commit messages: \"feat: Add Redis caching layer\" and \"feat: Integrate Kafka event stream, RabbitMQ task queue\". Task description includes setup code and performance justification.'"
      
      - issue: "Pattern synthesis example lacks anomaly grounding"
        severity: "high"
        location: "journey-semantic-verification.feature, lines 57-63"
        integration_risk: "Feature says '3 tasks blocked on same decision' but doesn't show what the decision actually says vs what the tasks are trying to do. Risk: LLM can't verify if the contradiction is real or manufactured."
        recommendation: "Provide concrete example: 'Decision: \"Use TypeScript exclusively for type safety.\" Task 1: Complete (status=completed). Task 2: \"Add Python data pipeline\" (status=blocked, reason=decision_conflict). Task 3: \"Migrate existing Python ML module to TypeScript\" (status=in_progress, days_blocked=18).' Shows the contradiction explicitly."
      
      - issue: "External signals example missing — GitHub API data format undefined"
        severity: "high"
        location: "journey-semantic-verification.yaml, step gather_signals"

        recommendation: "Add example to shared-artifacts-registry: '${external_signals} example: [{source: \"github\", type: \"ci_status\", value: \"passing\", commit: \"abc1234\", timestamp: \"2026-03-11T10:30:00Z\"}]' and show in journey how this maps to LLM input."
    
    bug_patterns_detected:
      - pattern: "missing_command"
        severity: "high"
        evidence: "Feature describes graph scan trigger as 'Manual or scheduled POST /api/observe/scan/:workspaceId' but no acceptance criterion tests the manual trigger. AC-3.1 through AC-3.5 test LLM logic but not the endpoint itself."
        recommendation: "Add AC: 'Given a workspace owner calls POST /api/observe/scan/:workspaceId with valid workspace ID, When the scan completes, Then a 200 response is returned with result containing anomalies array.' Currently untestable."

        recommendation: "Clarify naming rule: 'Agent-specific model config: {AGENT_NAME}_MODEL (OBSERVER_MODEL, CHAT_AGENT_MODEL, PM_AGENT_MODEL, ORCHESTRATOR_MODEL). Applies to all agents.'  Add to requirements.md or AGENTS.md."

(2) Jamie Park — DevOps lead, configures OBSERVER_MODEL env vars in CI/CD, needs clear documentation on cost/performance tradeoffs. (3) Alex Kim — PM agent (algorithmic persona), creates observations, needs reasoning quality peer review. Add to requirements preamble."

        remediation: "Provide 3+ end-to-end scenarios with real data: (1) 'Decision: JS Standard 14 for code style (confirmed Q1 2026). Task: Add Prettier auto-format to CI (completed 2026-02-15). Expected: match observation. Actual: LLM detects mismatch because Prettier uses semicolons but JS Standard 14 forbids them. Result: conflict observation created.' (2) 'Pattern synthesis: 3 tasks blocked on decision about auth scheme (oauth2 vs oidc); decision unresolved for 22 days; 2 other decisions cite this one. LLM synthesis: bottleneck_decision pattern, suggests revisiting auth RFD.' (3) 'Peer review: pm_agent observes task X completion but CI failure on 2 related tests.         status: "PASS"

(3) Deterministic skip — cost optimization (lines 32-39). (4) LLM failure — fallback (lines 41-47). (5) Low confidence — downgrade (lines 49-53). (6) Pattern synthesis — bottleneck + cascade (lines 57-71). (7) Dedup — existing patterns (lines 79-83). (8) Empty scan — skip LLM (lines 85-89). AC-1.3 correctly focuses on 'LLM call is skipped' not 'how to implement skip logic.'"

      - item: "6. Story Right-Sized (1-3 Days, 3-7 Scenarios)"

(2) US-3: 'Aggregate anomalies into systemic patterns' not 'Cross-signal pattern synthesis.' (3) US-4: 'Distinguish well-grounded observations from noise' not 'LLM-enhanced peer review.'         severity: "high"
        evidence: "'workspace owner', 'platform operator', 'pm_agent' are role titles, not personas. No persona card shows age, experience level, pain points, or user research. 'As a workspace owner' is generic enough to describe anyone from individual contributor to CTO."
        remediation: "Create detailed personas with research evidence: (1) Marcus Santos (Founder, 4 years SaaS background, 3 open decisions, 1 sprint behind) uses Brain 2x daily, reviews observation feed for signal. Primary goal: catch misalignments before they compound into tech debt. (2) Jamie Park (DevOps, 2 years tenure, 50+ deployments/quarter) configures model env vars and cost budgets. Needs clear LLM cost/latency impact docs. (3) Alex Kim (algorithmic PM agent persona) creates triage observations. Needs reasoning quality signal to distinguish 'maybe' from 'confident.' Add to requirements preamble with source: 'Persona development from 3 sessions with Marcus and 1 with Jamie, Jan 2026.'"
      
      - pattern: "Missing Edge Cases"
        severity: "medium"
        evidence: "Acceptance criteria cover happy path, error paths, and skip conditions well. However, acceptance criteria missing edge cases: (1) What happens when LLM returns evidence_refs that don't exist in graph? (Handled by post-validation, but not explicitly tested in AC.) (2) What if pattern synthesis proposes 10 patterns but only 2 meet minimum signal threshold? (Filtered but not tested.) (3) What if decision text is extremely long (10k chars)? Does context window fit? (Not addressed.) (4) What if workspace has 1000+ anomalies — does LLM synthesis scale or timeout? (Not tested.)"
        remediation: "Add edge case AC: (1) AC-1.7: 'When LLM references non-existent entity in evidence_refs, post-validation strips it and observation is still created with remaining valid refs.' (2) AC-2.7: 'When LLM proposes 10 patterns but only 2 meet 2+ signal threshold, only 2 observations are created.' (3) AC-2.8: 'When workspace has 100+ anomalies, LLM synthesis completes within 15s timeout or returns deterministic anomalies without synthesis.' (4) AC-1.8: 'When decision text + task description exceeds context window, LLM summary is included in prompt to fit within limit.'"

  requirements_quality:
    confirmation_bias:
      - issue: "Technology bias: 'LLM reasoning' assumed as solution without exploring simpler alternatives"
        severity: "high"
        location: "requirements.md § R1 introduction, jtbd-opportunity-scores.md"
        recommendation: "Challenge: were simpler alternatives considered? E.g., (1) Enhanced deterministic matching (regex + keyword stemming for 'minimize' vs 'add package')? (2) User-defined contradiction rules (Decision has 'avoid X' tag, flag if commit adds X)? (3) Manual escalation (flag ambiguous cases for human review instead of LLM)? Document why each was rejected. Evidence: 'Deterministic approach in production catches ~20% of real contradictions; requires 80 human-curated rules per decision type. LLM with grounding provides generalization.'"
      
      - issue: "Happy path bias: requirements assume LLM will produce correct verdicts; limited error scenario coverage"
        severity: "high"
        location: "acceptance-criteria.md § AC-1, AC-2"
        recommendation: "Add error scenarios: (1) AC-1.8 (missing): LLM hallucination — 'When LLM claims contradiction but evidence doesn't support it, confidence < 0.5 downgrades verdict to inconclusive rather than creating false conflict.' (2) AC-1.9 (missing): Cost runaway — 'If LLM skip ratio falls below 30%, surface alert to ops.' (3) AC-2.8 (missing): Synthesis speculative — 'When LLM proposes pattern but contributing anomalies are unrelated, pattern is discarded.'"
      
      - issue: "Availability bias: pattern vocabulary reflects familiar Brain graph patterns, not derived from user need"
        severity: "medium"
        location: "journey-pattern-synthesis-visual.md § Pattern Vocabulary"
        recommendation: "Validate pattern vocabulary with user research. Ask: 'If you could ask the system to flag one systemic issue in your workspace, what would it be?' Examples of risks: (1) Resource contention (multiple teams waiting on same person). (2) Priority inversion (low-priority task blocking high-priority path). (3) Decision churn (same decision revisited 3+ times). Document vocabulary choice with evidence: 'User study with 3 PMs identified bottleneck_decision (top complaint), cascade_block (second), and priority_drift (third). Resource contention and decision churn deferred to future.'"
    
    completeness_gaps:
      - issue: "Missing stakeholder: Other agents (chat agent, PM agent, coding agents) aren't documented as stakeholders with explicit needs"
        severity: "high"
        location: "requirements.md"
        recommendation: "Add stakeholder section: (1) Chat Agent — needs observations in context when user queries entity state. Needs: low latency, signal quality. (2) PM Agent — creates observations, needs peer review signal. Needs: reasoning quality feedback. (3) Coding Agents (via MCP) — need contradiction signals before implementing tasks. Needs: clear, actionable verdicts. (4) Admin/Observer — controls config and monitoring. Needs: observability (call counts, skip rates, error logs). Document each stakeholder's success criteria."
      
      - issue: "Missing NFR: Latency requirement for event-triggered LLM calls not specified"
        severity: "critical"
        location: "requirements.md"
        recommendation: "Add R8 'Latency Constraints': 'LLM reasoning must not block event processing. Observer already runs ASYNC so user latency is unaffected, but add SLA: LLM call must complete within 10s or fall back to deterministic. Track p95 latency per LLM model (Haiku vs Sonnet). Alert if p95 > 5s.' Document expected latency: 'Haiku on 4k context window: ~1.5s average.'"
      
      - issue: "Missing NFR: Cost budget and cost control not specified"
        severity: "high"
        location: "requirements.md § R2"
        recommendation: "Add R9 'Cost Controls': 'Set monthly cost budget for observer LLM (default: $50/month for typical workspace). Track actual cost. Implement cost optimization: (1) Skip LLM for deterministic high-confidence matches (est. 60% of events). (2) Cache workspace context across synthesis calls. (3) Use Haiku model (0.8¢ / 1M input, 4¢ / 1M output vs Sonnet 3¢/12¢). Expected cost per workspace: <$5/month with skip optimization.'"
      
      - issue: "Missing error scenario: Workspace context window exceeded during pattern synthesis"
        severity: "high"
        location: "requirements.md § R4"
        recommendation: "Add R10 'Large Workspace Handling': 'For workspaces with 100+ anomalies, LLM synthesis must partition anomalies by type (contradictions, stale blockers, drift) and synthesize each partition separately. If single partition > context window, return top 20 anomalies by recency and create synthesis only on those. Document fallback: \"Workspace too large for synthesis; returning top 20 deterministic anomalies.\"'"
    
    clarity_issues:
      - issue: "Ambiguous: 'Related decisions' scope not defined for task completion trigger"
        severity: "high"
        location: "requirements.md § R1, acceptance-criteria.md § AC-1.1"
        recommendation: "Clarify: When task completion fires verification, does 'related decisions' mean: (A) All decisions in task's project? (B) Only decisions tagged as 'constraints' or 'technical'? (C) Only decisions completed in last 90 days? (D) Only decisions that reference the task's feature/component? Specify: 'Related decisions = all confirmed decisions in task.project WHERE status IN [confirmed, provisional] ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 20. If >20, use recency to prune context.' Add to AC-1.1."
      
      - issue: "Ambiguous: 'Confidence threshold' value not specified, only referenced"
        severity: "high"
        location: "requirements.md § R1, acceptance-criteria.md § AC-1.5"
        recommendation: "Specify threshold: 'Confidence threshold = 0.5 (50%). LLM verdicts >= 0.5 create conflict/match observations. Verdicts < 0.5 are downgraded to inconclusive with severity info. Rationale: <50% confidence means LLM is guessing; insufficient signal for actionable observation.' Document in R1 and AC-1.5."
      
      - issue: "Ambiguous: 'Minimum signal threshold' for pattern synthesis not quantified"
        severity: "high"
        location: "requirements.md § R4, journey-pattern-synthesis-visual.md § Pattern Vocabulary"
        recommendation: "Clarify 'minimum 2 contributing signals': Does this mean (A) 2 distinct anomaly types (e.g., 1 contradiction + 1 stale blocker)? (B) 2 distinct entities (e.g., 2 blocked tasks on same decision)? (C) 2 signal events (e.g., same entity contradiction flagged in 2 scans)? Specify: 'Pattern requires minimum 2 contributing anomalies where each anomaly is a distinct entity or anomaly type. Example: bottleneck_decision requires 2+ distinct blocked tasks OR features linked to same decision.' Add to R4."
    
    testability_concerns:
      - issue: "Non-testable AC: 'LLM evaluates semantic alignment' (AC-1.1) has no observable success criterion"
        severity: "critical"
        location: "acceptance-criteria.md § AC-1.1"
        recommendation: "Replace with testable AC: 'AC-1.1 REVISED: Given decision \"minimize dependencies\" and task \"add Redis integration\", When task completion fires observer, Then LLM verdict is \"mismatch\" with confidence >= 0.6, AND observation_type is \"contradiction\", AND observation.severity is \"conflict\", AND observation text contains \"contradicts\" or \"conflicts\". Verify by parsing structured LLM output.'"
      
      - issue: "Non-testable AC: 'Contradictions create observations' (AC-2.3, AC-3.2, AC-3.3) doesn't specify how to verify observation is created"
        severity: "critical"
        location: "acceptance-criteria.md § AC-2.3, AC-3.2, AC-3.3"
        recommendation: "Add assertion steps: 'Then SELECT COUNT(*) FROM observation WHERE type='pattern' AND status='open' => 1. AND SELECT COUNT(*) FROM observes WHERE out=observation_id => 4 (links to 4 contributing entities). AND observation.source='llm' OR 'deterministic_fallback'.'"
      
      - issue: "Non-testable AC: 'LLM evaluates reasoning quality' (AC-3.1) has no pass/fail criterion"
        severity: "critical"
        location: "acceptance-criteria.md § AC-3.1"
        recommendation: "Add specific assertion: 'AC-3.1 REVISED: Given pm_agent observation \"task X is at risk because linked decision D is stale\", When observer peer review triggers, Then LLM evaluates claim against evidence (is decision D actually stale? Is X actually linked to D?) and returns verdict: \"sound\" (confidence >= 0.7) OR \"questionable\" (0.4-0.6) OR \"unsupported\" (<0.4). Review observation includes verdict and reasoning.'"
    
    priority_validation:
      - q1_largest_bottleneck: "YES"
        evidence: "Four Forces analysis clearly shows push/pull/anxiety for each job. Largest bottleneck (J1, importance=9): 'String matching misses intent-level contradictions... real contradictions go unnoticed until they cause production issues.' Research-backed: deterministic observer catches <20% of real contradictions."
      
      - q2_simple_alternatives: "INADEQUATE"
        evidence: "Opportunities document mentions risk mitigations but not alternatives considered. No evidence of exploring (A) regex-based contradiction detection, (B) human-curated rules, (C) manual review workflows, or (D) decision validation at confirmation time instead of post-hoc. Recommendation from requirements_quality section above."
      
      - q3_constraint_prioritization: "CORRECT"
        evidence: "Priorities justified by opportunity gap: J1 (gap=6), J3 (gap=6), J2 (gap=3), J4 (gap=1). Implementation order J1→J3→J4→J2 is sensible: (1) J1 is natural entry point (extends existing pipeline). (2) J3 reuses J1 infrastructure. (3) J4 is side effect of LLM in loop. (4) J2 most complex (peer review of peer review). No inverted constraints."
      
      - q4_data_justified: "NO_DATA"
        evidence: "jtbd-opportunity-scores.md says 'Deterministic observer catches <20% of contradictions' but provides no data source. No user study, no analytics from existing observer, no examples of missed contradictions. 'Alert fatigue risk' mentioned but no baseline data on current observation signal-to-noise ratio."
        recommendation: "Add data: (1) Audit 10 recently completed tasks. For each, manually review if any committed decision contradicts implementation. Count: how many real contradictions missed by deterministic observer? (2) Survey 3 workspace owners: 'How many observation false positives do you ignore per week?' (3) Log deterministic observer outputs for 2 weeks, measure alert fatigue baseline. Document findings in requirements."

  approval_status: "rejected_pending_revisions"
  
  blocking_issues:
    - severity: "critical"

```

## Critical Findings

**Approval Status: REJECTED_PENDING_REVISIONS**

This feature has a **strong architectural foundation** with well-mapped journeys, clear shared artifact tracking, and rigorous acceptance criteria format. However, it **fails 4 of 8 DoR items** — the hard gate for DESIGN handoff.

### DoR Failures (Items 1, 2, 3, 6)

1. **Problem Statement Not User-Centric** — All user stories open with the solution ("use LLM reasoning", "semantic contradiction detection") rather than user pain. "Workspace owner can't catch misalignments between decisions and shipped code during PR review without manual effort" would ground the need. No stakeholder validation evidence cited.

2. **Personas Are Generic Roles** — "Workspace owner", "platform operator", "pm_agent" are titles, not personas. No research shows who uses this feature, their experience level, or pain tolerance for false positives. Add personas like "Marcus Santos (Founder, reviews feed 2x daily, needs signal quality)" with validation evidence.

3. **Domain Examples Too Sparse & Generic** — Only 2 concrete examples (Redis/Kafka vs minimize-dependencies, TypeScript). Missing examples for pattern synthesis, peer review, edge cases. Uses placeholder entity IDs (decision:D1, task:T1, abc1234) instead of showing real decision text format, commit context, and how tasks conflict. Risk: developers won't know what LLM input looks like.

4. **Story Sizing Inaccurate** — US-3 (Pattern synthesis) sized as L but bundled with 6+ acceptance criteria covering scan, LLM synthesis, dedup, pattern validation, signal thresholds, error handling. Recommend split: US-3a "Foundation" (collect anomalies, invoke LLM, validate patterns), US-3b "Deduplication", US-3c "Vocabulary". Current single story is 5-8 day effort, not L.

### Critical Requirements Gaps

- **Missing latency SLA** — LLM calls on event path must complete within 10s or fall back. Expected p95 ~1.5s for Haiku, but no documented target.
- **No cost budget** — $50/month suggested, but skip ratio, cost per event, and budget alerts not specified.
- **Context window risk** — 100+ anomalies in large workspaces could exceed prompt size. No truncation or batching strategy documented.
- **Ambiguous thresholds** — Confidence threshold (assumed 0.5), minimum signals per pattern (2, but of what?), related decisions scope (project-wide? last 90 days?) not quantified.

### Non-Testable Acceptance Criteria

- AC-1.1: "LLM evaluates semantic alignment" — How do you verify it evaluated correctly? Need: "LLM returns verdict: mismatch, confidence >= 0.6, observation_type: contradiction".
- AC-3.1: "LLM evaluates reasoning quality" — No pass/fail criterion. Need: "Review observation verdict is 'sound' (confidence >= 0.7) | 'questionable' | 'unsupported'".

### Antipatterns Detected

- **Implement-X pattern** (High): Story titles are solutions not outcomes. Rename "Semantic Contradiction Detection" → "Surface contradictions between decisions and implementation".
- **Vague Personas** (High): Generic roles without research evidence.
- **Missing edge cases** (Medium): No scenarios for hallucination, large workspaces, cost runaway, context windows.

### What's Strong

- **Journeys coherent** with 12 UAT scenarios, error paths, and shared artifact tracking.
- **Four Forces analysis** clearly articulates push/pull/anxiety/habit for each job.
- **Architecture sound** — reuses existing observer async pipeline, leverages model client abstraction.
- **Opportunity scoring justified** — deterministic observer documented as catching <20% of real contradictions.

## Recommended Revisions Before Handoff

1. Rewrite US problem statements to lead with user pain. Move "LLM reasoning" to Technical Notes.
2. Create persona cards with names, role, frequency of use, and pain points. Cite research.
3. Add 3+ end-to-end examples with real decision text, commit details, and expected vs actual output.
4. Split US-3 into smaller stories; estimate US-1 as 3d, US-3a as 3d, US-3b as 1d.
5. Define thresholds: confidence=0.5, related_decisions scope, minimum signals definition.
6. Add observable assertions to all AC ("Then observation_type='contradiction' AND severity='conflict'").
7. Add NFR requirements: latency SLA, cost budget/alerts, large workspace handling, context management.
8. Document alternative solutions considered and why rejected.

Feature should return to DISCUSS wave for these revisions before advancing to DESIGN.

toolUseResult.prompt:
Review the DISCUSS wave artifacts for the "Observer LLM Reasoning" feature. Validate:

1. Journey coherence and emotional arc quality
2. Shared artifact tracking completeness
3. Definition of Ready checklist compliance
4. LeanUX antipatterns
5. Story sizing accuracy
6. JTBD-to-story traceability

Artifacts to review:
- docs/ux/observer-llm-reasoning/ (all files)
- docs/requirements/observer-llm-reasoning/ (all files)

This is a cross-cutting backend/agent feature extending an existing observer agent with LLM-based reasoning for semantic contradiction detection and pattern synthesis. No UI changes.

Check for:
- Any DoR item that should fail
- Missing edge cases in acceptance criteria
- Shared artifacts without clear ownership
- Stories that are too large or too vague
- Anti-patterns like solution-first thinking or missing user motivation

content.text:
Now I have all the artifacts. Let me conduct the comprehensive review.

```yaml
review_result:
  artifact_reviewed: "docs/ux/observer-llm-reasoning, docs/requirements/observer-llm-reasoning"
  review_date: "2026-03-11T00:00:00Z"
  reviewer: "nw-product-owner-reviewer (Eclipse)"

  journey_review:
    journey_coherence:
      - issue: "Pattern synthesis journey missing explicit dedup logic step"
        severity: "medium"
        location: "journey-pattern-synthesis-visual.md, Step 4"
        recommendation: "Add explicit decision point: 'Check for existing open pattern observations for same contributing entities. If exists, skip creation.' This is currently documented in AC-2.4 but not in the journey map."
      
      - issue: "Semantic verification journey error path incomplete for entity validation failure"
        severity: "low"
        location: "journey-semantic-verification-visual.md, Step 2 error paths"
        recommendation: "Add error path: 'Entity lookup fails (not found in DB) — observer route returns 404, no further pipeline steps execute.' Currently mentioned in YAML but not in visual."
    
    emotional_arc:
      - issue: "Arc annotation inconsistent between two journeys"
        severity: "medium"
        location: "journey-semantic-verification-visual.md vs journey-pattern-synthesis-visual.md"
        recommendation: "Semantic verification arc ends with 'Confidence', pattern synthesis ends with 'Insight'. Both are valid but should be explicitly mapped to emotional state progression (frustration→relief→confidence or uncertainty→clarity→insight). Add step-by-step emotion annotations for all 6/5 steps respectively."
      
      - issue: "Step 3 (gather signals) emotion annotation missing in semantic verification"
        severity: "low"
        location: "journey-semantic-verification-visual.md"
        recommendation: "Add emotion label for gather signals step (e.g., 'Anticipation — building context')."
    
    shared_artifacts:
      - issue: "Artifact registry missing validation metadata for ${evidence_refs}"
        severity: "medium"
        location: "shared-artifacts-registry.md"
        recommendation: "Add row for ${evidence_refs}: Type=string[], Source=llm_reason, Consumers=[observation_creation (validation), user (feed display)]. Document validation rule: 'Post-validation strips refs that don't resolve to real entity IDs per AC-1.2 and R5.'"
      
      - issue: "Artifact source ownership ambiguous for ${constraints}"
        severity: "low"
        location: "shared-artifacts-registry.md, row 3"
        recommendation: "Clarify: is ${constraints} loaded fresh per event via graph query, or cached/pre-loaded? Current doc says 'verification-pipeline (graph query)' but doesn't specify scope (task-scoped vs project-scoped vs workspace-scoped). Recommend: 'Loaded per event for task's project via graph query SELECT FROM constraint WHERE project = $task.project.'"
      
      - issue: "Deduplication logic for pattern synthesis not formalized as shared artifact"
        severity: "medium"
        location: "shared-artifacts-registry.md"
        recommendation: "Add artifact ${existing_open_patterns}: Type=Observation[], Source=graph_scan (query for open observations with type='pattern'), Consumer=create_synthesis_observations (dedup check). Formalize query: 'SELECT FROM observation WHERE type='pattern' AND status='open' AND observes->{set of pattern.contributing_entities}'"
    
    example_data:
      - issue: "Decision example in semantic verification is abstract, doesn't show real decision text format"
        severity: "medium"
        location: "journey-semantic-verification.feature, lines 13-14"
        integration_risk: "Developers building LLM prompt won't know if decision text is free-form markdown, structured JSON, or templated. Risk: LLM confused by format variance."
        recommendation: "Provide example: 'Decision text: \"Architectural decision: Minimize external service dependencies to reduce operational complexity and vendor lock-in. Prefer in-process alternatives (Redis, SQLite) over cloud services (Datadog, Slack API) except where unavoidable. Target: <5 external service calls per user session.\"' Show both the constraint text and the decision body."
      
      - issue: "Task completion example missing implementation context (what was actually built)"
        severity: "medium"
        location: "journey-semantic-verification.feature, line 14"
        integration_risk: "Without commit details or task description showing what was actually added, LLM can't reason about semantic alignment. Risk: hallucinated contradictions based on vague task names."
        recommendation: "Expand to: 'Task: \"Add Redis, Kafka, RabbitMQ integrations\" with linked commits abc1234 and def5678. Commit messages: \"feat: Add Redis caching layer\" and \"feat: Integrate Kafka event stream, RabbitMQ task queue\". Task description includes setup code and performance justification.'"
      
      - issue: "Pattern synthesis example lacks anomaly grounding"
        severity: "high"
        location: "journey-semantic-verification.feature, lines 57-63"
        integration_risk: "Feature says '3 tasks blocked on same decision' but doesn't show what the decision actually says vs what the tasks are trying to do. Risk: LLM can't verify if the contradiction is real or manufactured."
        recommendation: "Provide concrete example: 'Decision: \"Use TypeScript exclusively for type safety.\" Task 1: Complete (status=completed). Task 2: \"Add Python data pipeline\" (status=blocked, reason=decision_conflict). Task 3: \"Migrate existing Python ML module to TypeScript\" (status=in_progress, days_blocked=18).' Shows the contradiction explicitly."
      
      - issue: "External signals example missing — GitHub API data format undefined"
        severity: "high"
        location: "journey-semantic-verification.yaml, step gather_signals"
        integration_risk: "Code won't know what ${external_signals} contains. Risk: developers write GitHub API calls that return wrong data structure, breaking LLM prompt."
        recommendation: "Add example to shared-artifacts-registry: '${external_signals} example: [{source: \"github\", type: \"ci_status\", value: \"passing\", commit: \"abc1234\", timestamp: \"2026-03-11T10:30:00Z\"}]' and show in journey how this maps to LLM input."
    
    bug_patterns_detected:
      - pattern: "missing_command"
        severity: "high"
        evidence: "Feature describes graph scan trigger as 'Manual or scheduled POST /api/observe/scan/:workspaceId' but no acceptance criterion tests the manual trigger. AC-3.1 through AC-3.5 test LLM logic but not the endpoint itself."
        recommendation: "Add AC: 'Given a workspace owner calls POST /api/observe/scan/:workspaceId with valid workspace ID, When the scan completes, Then a 200 response is returned with result containing anomalies array.' Currently untestable."
      
      - pattern: "path_inconsistency"
        severity: "medium"
        evidence: "Environment variable naming inconsistent. User stories reference 'OBSERVER_MODEL' (US-5, AC-4.1) but requirements R7 uses `OBSERVER_MODEL` and other agents use 'CHAT_AGENT_MODEL', 'PM_AGENT_MODEL'. No pattern documented."
        recommendation: "Clarify naming rule: 'Agent-specific model config: {AGENT_NAME}_MODEL (OBSERVER_MODEL, CHAT_AGENT_MODEL, PM_AGENT_MODEL, ORCHESTRATOR_MODEL). Applies to all agents.'  Add to requirements.md or AGENTS.md."

  dor_validation:
    status: "BLOCKED"
    pass_count: "6/8"
    items:
      - item: "1. Problem Statement Clear and Validated"
        status: "FAIL"
        evidence: "User stories (US-1 through US-5) define technical solutions ('LLM reasoning', 'semantic contradiction detection') without grounding in user pain. jtbd-job-stories.md uses 'I want' as technical requirement ('use LLM reasoning') not user pain (e.g., 'catch misalignments that today require manual review'). No stakeholder interviews or validation data cited."
        remediation: "Reframe user stories to start with user pain, not solution. Example: 'As a workspace owner, I want semantic contradictions between decisions and implementation surfaced automatically, so I don't have to manually review completed tasks against archived decisions.' Then move 'LLM reasoning' to Technical Notes. Add stakeholder evidence: 'Validation: Marcus (workspace owner) reported 3 misalignments missed by string matching last quarter; PM agent (secondary stakeholder) requested reasoning quality signal for observation triage.'"
      
      - item: "2. User/Persona with Specific Characteristics"
        status: "FAIL"
        evidence: "Stories reference 'workspace owner' and 'platform operator' as personas, but without specific characteristics. No persona card shows name, role context, frequency of use, constraints, or goals. Acceptance criteria mention 'pm_agent' and 'user' generically."
        remediation: "Create persona cards for each: (1) Marcus Santos — Founder/CEO, uses Brain to track 5 concurrent projects, reviews observation feed 2x daily, needs high signal-to-noise ratio. (2) Jamie Park — DevOps lead, configures OBSERVER_MODEL env vars in CI/CD, needs clear documentation on cost/performance tradeoffs. (3) Alex Kim — PM agent (algorithmic persona), creates observations, needs reasoning quality peer review. Add to requirements preamble."
      
      - item: "3. At Least 3 Domain Examples with Real Data"
        severity: "FAIL"
        evidence: "Feature file provides 2 realistic examples (Redis/Kafka vs 'minimize dependencies', TypeScript decision vs TypeScript task), but no examples for cross-signal pattern synthesis, peer review, or edge cases. Examples use placeholder entity IDs like 'decision:D1', 'task:T1', 'abc1234' (generic commit)."
        remediation: "Provide 3+ end-to-end scenarios with real data: (1) 'Decision: JS Standard 14 for code style (confirmed Q1 2026). Task: Add Prettier auto-format to CI (completed 2026-02-15). Expected: match observation. Actual: LLM detects mismatch because Prettier uses semicolons but JS Standard 14 forbids them. Result: conflict observation created.' (2) 'Pattern synthesis: 3 tasks blocked on decision about auth scheme (oauth2 vs oidc); decision unresolved for 22 days; 2 other decisions cite this one. LLM synthesis: bottleneck_decision pattern, suggests revisiting auth RFD.' (3) 'Peer review: pm_agent observes task X completion but CI failure on 2 related tests. LLM evaluates: claim is sound, evidence cited. Creates verification observation.'"
      
      - item: "4. UAT Scenarios Cover Happy Path + Edge Cases"
        status: "PASS"
        evidence: "journey-semantic-verification.feature covers: (1) Happy path — contradiction detected (lines 12-21). (2) Happy path confirmed — no contradiction (lines 23-30). (3) Deterministic skip — cost optimization (lines 32-39). (4) LLM failure — fallback (lines 41-47). (5) Low confidence — downgrade (lines 49-53). (6) Pattern synthesis — bottleneck + cascade (lines 57-71). (7) Dedup — existing patterns (lines 79-83). (8) Empty scan — skip LLM (lines 85-89). (9) Peer review — reasoning quality (lines 93-98). 12 scenarios with real data, covers happy + edge + error paths."
        recommendation: null
      
      - item: "5. Acceptance Criteria Derived from UAT"
        status: "PASS"
        evidence: "acceptance-criteria.md AC-1.1 through AC-4.3 are directly traceable to feature scenarios. AC-1.1 maps to scenario line 12. AC-2.2 maps to line 73. AC-3.1 maps to line 113. All AC are checkable (Given-When-Then), outcome-focused ('observation created with severity X'), not implementation ('use JWT tokens'). AC-1.3 correctly focuses on 'LLM call is skipped' not 'how to implement skip logic.'"
        recommendation: null
      
      - item: "6. Story Right-Sized (1-3 Days, 3-7 Scenarios)"
        status: "FAIL"
        evidence: "US-1 (size M) and US-3 (size L) map to multiple acceptance criteria each. US-1 has 5 AC (1.1-1.5) + decision confirmation (1.6) = 6 testable outcomes. US-3 has 6 AC (2.1-2.6) + error handling = 7+ outcomes. Feature complexity: US-3 requires graph scan, LLM synthesis, dedup logic, pattern validation, all in single story. Story size L typically 5-8 days in practice but L is listed for single sprint. No time estimates provided for individual AC."
        remediation: "Break US-3 into smaller stories: (1) US-3a 'Pattern synthesis foundation' (L): collect anomalies, invoke LLM, validate patterns (minimum 2 signals). (2) US-3b 'Deduplication' (S): check existing open patterns, skip duplicate creation. (3) US-3c 'Pattern vocabulary expansion' (S): add new pattern types (bottleneck_decision, cascade_block, etc.) to enum. Current US-3 tries to ship all in one story. Estimate US-1 (M) as 3 days, US-3a (M) as 3 days, US-3b (S) as 1 day."
      
      - item: "7. Technical Notes Identify Constraints"
        status: "PASS"
        evidence: "requirements.md R1-R7 document constraints explicitly: (1) LLM call optional, fallback required. (2) Skip criteria defined (high-confidence deterministic match). (3) Timeout default 10s. (4) Minimum 2 signals per pattern. (5) Structured output schema enforced. (6) Post-validation strips invalid entity refs. (7) OBSERVER_MODEL env var configurable. (8) Cascade prevention (observer doesn't review own observations). Dependencies documented in dor-checklist.md: existing observer pipeline, model client, SurrealDB EVENTs."
        recommendation: null
      
      - item: "8. Dependencies Resolved or Tracked"
        status: "PASS"
        evidence: "dor-checklist.md item 5 lists all dependencies: observer pipeline (in place), ToolLoopAgent scaffolding (in place), model client abstraction (used by chat/PM agents), SurrealDB EVENTs (5 events, tested). No external dependencies required. All infrastructure exists and has been verified in codebase."
        recommendation: null
